content_of_post_to_comment: We tend to reject each other as women when one of us feels anger. It scares us. What could it mean if our own anger is valid? What long held beliefs might we have to let go of? It is all too scary so most of us put the lid back on it and call it a day. Sadly we often push away our sisters in the process. We reinforce patriarchal conditioning that says ‘don’t look at it, fall into line.’
comment: I wonder if the harder part isn't recognizing anger but deciding what to do with it. Suppression protects the status quo, but expression without reflection can recreate the same dynamics in a different form.
